Content: Long distance isn't for the weak. On my quiet days, I wish we didn't have to deal with time zones, missed calls, and empty hugs. I wish we could go on random dates, hold each other during rough days, or just exist side by side.But more than the ache of distance, I carry this bigger kind of love - the kind that still roots for you even when you're far away. I want to see you win, to be healthy, happy, and fulfilled. Because love isn't just about being together physically. It's about growing, sacrificing, and holding on to hope - that one day, all this distance will turn into a home we finally share????????

Content: 2 years done 2 more to go, 8000km / 5000 miles apart and for the first time we also have 7hr difference and god i hate it but my man is worth it. it gets easier.. i cry when we are about to leave again but then you get used to a routine. we do discord calls & share meals & watch shows &play games & fall asleep together and stay on the call for as long as we can just sit in silence or literally anything. now with time difference it's much harder but we still try to talk at least for 30min-1h per day on call & we text through the day and update each other. it's hard but worth it every second

Content: Its the hardest thing Ive ever had to go through. Keep yourself busy, suround yourself with people that bring you joy, do as many things as you can that bring you joy. But at the end of the day itll be hard. Allow yourself to cry and to feel the sadness. Tell him about it and let him comfort you. You dont wanna go through the darkness alone.
